briefdescription.para: Construct a new Path object with default properties. Initializes an empty path with the closed property set to false.
Construct a new Path object with random positions and values. Initializes a path with a specified number of points, random values, and the option to be open or closed.

parameterdescription.para: Number of points to generate.
Random seed number for generating random values.
Bounding box for random point generation.

briefdescription.para: Construct a new Path object based on a list of points. Initializes a path with the specified points and an option to be open or closed.

parameterdescription.para: List of points defining the path.

briefdescription.para: Construct a new Path object based on x and y coordinates. Initializes a path with the specified x and y coordinates and an option to be open or closed.

parameterdescription.para: List of x coordinates for the points.
List of y coordinates for the points.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::vector< float > x, std::vector< float > y, std::vector< float > v)
memberdef.name: Path

briefdescription.para: Construct a new Path object based on x, y coordinates, and values. Initializes a path with the specified x and y coordinates, associated values, and an option to be open or closed.

parameterdescription.para: List of x coordinates for the points.
List of y coordinates for the points.
List of values associated with the points.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< glm::ivec2 > &indices, const glm::ivec2 &shape, const glm::vec4 &bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f})
memberdef.name: Path

briefdescription.para: Construct a point cloud from grid indices mapped to a bounding box.

detaileddescription.para: Each index is normalized by the grid shape and remapped to the given bounding box. The resulting points are stored as 3D positions (z = 1).

parameterdescription.para: Input grid indices.
Grid dimensions.
Bounding box (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax). Default is {0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f}.

briefdescription.para: Constructs a new Path object from lists of xyz data as glm::vec3.
Get the arc length of the path.

detaileddescription.para: The arc length is the cumulative distance along the path, normalized to the range [0, 1]. This represents the distance traveled from the start to each point along the path as a fraction of the total path length.

simplesect.para: std::vector<float> Vector of arc length values, where each entry corresponds to a point on the path and represents the normalized distance from the start of the path to that point.

briefdescription.para: Get the cumulative distance of the path.

detaileddescription.para: This method computes the cumulative distance along the path, which is the total distance traveled up to each point on the path. It accumulates the distances from the start of the path to each point.

simplesect.para: std::vector<float> Vector of cumulative distance values, where each entry represents the distance from the start of the path to the respective point.

briefdescription.para: Computes the signed curvature at each point of the path.

parameterdescription.para: If true, scales values to [-1, 1] using the maximum absolute curvature.

briefdescription.para: Compute midpoints of path edges.

detaileddescription.para: Returns the midpoint of each segment between consecutive points.

briefdescription.para: Computes unit normals at each point of the path.
Computes unit tangents at each point of the path.
Get the values assigned to the points on the path.

detaileddescription.para: This method retrieves the values assigned to each point on the path. These values can represent any attribute associated with the points, such as color, intensity, or other metrics.

simplesect.para: std::vector<float> Vector of values assigned to the points on the path.

briefdescription.para: Get the x coordinates of the points on the path.

detaileddescription.para: This method retrieves the x coordinates of all points in the path. Each value in the returned vector corresponds to the x coordinate of a point along the path.

simplesect.para: std::vector<float> Vector of x values of the points on the path.

briefdescription.para: Get the coordinates of the points as a single vector.

detaileddescription.para: This method returns the coordinates of the points in the path as a single vector, where the coordinates are interleaved: (x0, y0, x1, y1, ...). This format is useful for operations that require a flat representation of the coordinates.

simplesect.para: std::vector<float> Vector of interleaved x and y coordinates of the points on the path.

briefdescription.para: Get the y coordinates of the points on the path.

detaileddescription.para: This method retrieves the y coordinates of all points in the path. Each value in the returned vector corresponds to the y coordinate of a point along the path.

simplesect.para: std::vector<float> Vector of y values of the points on the path.

briefdescription.para: Check whether the path is closed.

simplesect.para: True if the path is closed, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Set whether the path is closed.

parameterdescription.para: True to close the path, false to keep it open.

briefdescription.para: Clear the path data.

detaileddescription.para: This method removes all points and associated data from the path, effectively resetting it to an empty state.

briefdescription.para: Enforces monotonicity on the values of the points in the path.

detaileddescription.para: This method adjusts the v values of the points in the path to ensure that they are either monotonically decreasing or increasing, based on the input parameter.

parameterdescription.para: If true, enforces a monotonically decreasing order for the values. If false, enforces a monotonically increasing order for the values.

simplesect.para: This method modifies the path in place.

briefdescription.para: Reorder points using a nearest neighbor search.

detaileddescription.para: This method reorders the points in the path to minimize the total distance by performing a nearest neighbor search starting from the specified start_index. This approach is useful for optimizing the path or improving its order.

parameterdescription.para: Index of the starting point for the nearest neighbor search. Default is 0.

briefdescription.para: Reverse the order of points in the path.

detaileddescription.para: This method reverses the sequence of points in the path, which can be useful for various applications, such as changing the direction of the path traversal.

briefdescription.para: Divide the path by adding a point between each pair of consecutive points.

detaileddescription.para: This method adds new points in the middle of each segment of the path to create a denser set of points along the path. This is useful for increasing the resolution of the path.

briefdescription.para: Resample the path to achieve an approximately constant distance between points.

detaileddescription.para: This method adjusts the points in the path to ensure that the distance between each consecutive point is approximately equal to the specified delta. This is useful for creating a path with evenly spaced points.

parameterdescription.para: Target distance between consecutive points.
Interpolation method used to resample the path.

briefdescription.para: Resamples the path using cubic interpolation along arc length.

detaileddescription.para: The path is reparameterized by arc length and interpolated using cubic splines for x, y, and value components.

parameterdescription.para: Number of points to resample.
Interpolation method to use (default is CUBIC).

briefdescription.para: Resample the path to achieve fairly uniform distance between consecutive points.

detaileddescription.para: This method adjusts the path so that the distance between each consecutive point is as uniform as possible. It redistributes the points to ensure more even spacing along the path.

parameterdescription.para: Interpolation method used for resampling.

briefdescription.para: Subsample the path by keeping only every n-th point.

detaileddescription.para: This method reduces the number of points in the path by retaining only every 'step'-th point, effectively subsampling the path. This can be useful for simplifying the path or reducing data size.

parameterdescription.para: The interval of points to keep. For example, a step of 2 will keep every second point.

briefdescription.para: Project path points to an array.

detaileddescription.para: This method projects the points of the path onto a 2D array, filling the array based on the path's points. Optionally, the contour of the path can be filled using flood fill if the filled parameter is set to true.

parameterdescription.para: The array to which the path points will be projected.
Bounding box defining the domain of the array.
Boolean flag indicating whether to perform flood filling of the path's contour.

memberdef.argsstring: (glm::ivec2 shape, glm::vec4 bbox={0.f, 1.f, 0.f, 1.f}, bool filled=false) const
memberdef.name: to_array

briefdescription.para: See hmap::to_array.
See hmap::to_array.
Export path as PNG image file.

detaileddescription.para: This method generates a PNG image representing the path and saves it to the specified file. The resolution of the image can be adjusted using the shape parameter.

parameterdescription.para: The filename for the output PNG image.
Resolution of the image, specified as width and height. Default is {512, 512}.

briefdescription.para: Represents an ordered set of points in 2D, forming a polyline (open or closed).

detaileddescription.para: The Path class extends the Cloud class to represent a sequence of points in 2D space that are connected in a specific order, forming a polyline. The polyline can be either open or closed, depending on whether the first and last points are connected. This class provides methods for various geometric operations, including path length calculation, point insertion, and more.
